user_query:
I run a tiny neighborhood movie night on a shoestring, and I idolize Roger Ebert. Please coordinate across my local files to propose next screening candidates and write a concise status update I can share with the group.

Inputs (all relative to the workspace):
- input/watchlist.csv — my backlog with priorities and whether I’ve already seen something.
- input/ebert_great_movies.html — a locally saved snippet listing a handful of Ebert’s Great Movies.
- input/ebert_notes.md — my clipped notes with short Ebert quote snippets by film.
- input/viewing_log.jsonl — what I’ve actually watched, with dates and ratings.

Deliverables:
1) output/next_candidates.csv — a structured list of candidate films to screen next. Include only films that:
   - appear in input/watchlist.csv with priority = "high",
   - have no seen_date in the watchlist (i.e., not yet watched), and
   - also appear in input/ebert_great_movies.html (treat that as the authority on Ebert’s endorsement).
   Match by title case-insensitively and use the year to disambiguate when present. Columns (in this order):
   - title (string)
   - year (integer)
   - runtime_min (integer from watchlist)
   - priority (string from watchlist)
   - has_ebert_quote (boolean: true if a matching film section exists in input/ebert_notes.md)
   Sort rows by runtime_min ascending.

2) output/club_update.md — a brief, readable status update with the following sections:
   - Summary: counts for (a) total items on the watchlist, (b) number of high-priority unwatched items, and (c) how many of those high-priority unwatched also appear in the Ebert list.
   - Watched recently: the two most recent entries from input/viewing_log.jsonl with date, title, year, and rating.
   - Candidate picks: a list mirroring output/next_candidates.csv (same order), showing title (year) and runtime. If a matching section exists in input/ebert_notes.md, include the first sentence of the quote; otherwise note that a quote is missing.
   - Data checks: list any titles that appear in input/viewing_log.jsonl but are missing from input/watchlist.csv, and any titles that have a seen_date in the watchlist but do not appear in the viewing log. Use case-insensitive title matching and year to disambiguate.

Notes:
- Do not use the Internet; rely solely on the provided files.
- Normalize titles by trimming whitespace and comparing case-insensitively; compare years as integers when available.
- The HTML list in input/ebert_great_movies.html should be parsed to extract (title, year) pairs.
- The Ebert quote in input/ebert_notes.md should be matched by the section header of the form "## Title (Year)" and the first sentence of the following blockquote should be used.
- Ensure both outputs are written exactly to the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
